TextENGNew: Do you still wish to dream?
Is there still something you want?
Is there still someone you wish to ask something of?
This world may not take the shape you desired.
changeReason: New translation captures 姿 (shape/form) from the Japanese, specifying the world may not 'take the shape' you desired, rather than just 'not necessarily what you desired.'

TextENGNew: The people living here may not be the ones you have hoped for and given your heart to.
changeReason: New translation adds 'given your heart to' from JPN 心を寄せてきた, conveying emotional attachment missing in the original.

TextENGNew: And there is no guarantee that this ending is the one you dreamed of.
Even so — do you choose to move forward?
Even knowing it means casting aside the happiness of the present, turning back the hands of time?
Do you choose to face that anguish, that painful hardship, head-on...?
That strength of will — that is what we have been seeking from you.
Together with you, we will open the way to the future.
And this time — this time, we will seize happiness.
Pray that we may truly reach the happy future that everyone has yearned for and never been able to grasp...!
changeReason: New translation shifts perspective from third person ('everyone can reach') to first person plural ('we may truly reach'), changing the speaker's involvement in the prayer.
